briefdescription.para: IP protocol (pseudo-val for setsockopt()
ICMP protocol
IGMP protocol
Every packet.

detaileddescription.para: from linux if_ether.h

briefdescription.para: IPIP tunnels
TCP protocol
UDP protocol
IPv6 protocol
ICMPv6 protocol.
RAW IP packets
Protocol numbers from IANA/BSD.
TLS 1.0 protocol.
TLS 1.1 protocol.
TLS 1.2 protocol.
TLS 1.3 protocol.
DTLS 1.0 protocol.
DTLS 1.2 protocol.
Protocol numbers for TLS protocols.
Stream socket type
Datagram socket type.
RAW socket type
Socket type.
IPv6 MTU length.

detaileddescription.para: We must be able to receive this size IPv6 packet without fragmentation.

briefdescription.para: IPv4 MTU length.

detaileddescription.para: We must be able to receive this size IPv4 packet without fragmentation.

briefdescription.para: IP Maximum Transfer Unit.
Background (lowest)
Best effort (default)
Excellent effort
Critical applications
Video, < 100 ms latency and jitter.
Voice, < 10 ms latency and jitter
Internetwork control
Network control (highest)
Network packet priority settings described in IEEE 802.1Q Annex I.1.
Default (invalid) address type.
Tentative address
Preferred address
Deprecated address
What is the current state of the network address.
Default value.

detaileddescription.para: This is not a valid value.

briefdescription.para: Auto configured address.
Address is from DHCP.
Manually set address.
Manually set address which is overridable by DHCP.
How the network address is assigned to network interface.
Socket address family type.
Length of a socket address.
Check if the IPv6 address is a loopback address (::1).

simplesect.para: True if address is a loopback address, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a multicast address.

simplesect.para: True if address is multicast address, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if IPv6 address is found in one of the network interfaces.

simplesect.para: True if address was found, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if IPv6 multicast address is found in one of the network interfaces.

simplesect.para: True if address was found, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if two IPv6 addresses are same when compared after prefix mask.

simplesect.para: True if IPv6 prefixes are the same, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the IPv6 network address via the unicast address and the prefix mask.
Check if the IPv4 address is a loopback address (127.0.0.0/8).

simplesect.para: True if address is a loopback address, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv4 address is unspecified (all bits zero)

simplesect.para: True if the address is unspecified, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv4 address is a multicast address.

simplesect.para: True if address is multicast address, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv4 address is a link local address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv4 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv4 address is from a private address range.

detaileddescription.para: See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reserved_IP_addresses for details.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv4 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Copy an IPv4 address raw buffer.
Copy an IPv6 address raw buffer.
Compare two raw IPv4 address buffers.

simplesect.para: True if the addresses are the same, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Compare two IPv4 addresses.

simplesect.para: True if the addresses are the same, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Compare two IPv6 addresses.

simplesect.para: True if the addresses are the same, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Compare two raw IPv6 address buffers.

simplesect.para: True if the addresses are the same, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv6 address is a link local address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv6 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv6 address is a site local address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv6 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv6 address is a unique local address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv6 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv6 address is a global address.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv6 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv6 address is from a private/local address range.

detaileddescription.para: See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reserved_IP_addresses for details.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv6 address

simplesect.para: True if it is, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Return pointer to any (all bits zeros) IPv6 address.

simplesect.para: Any IPv6 address.

briefdescription.para: Return pointer to any (all bits zeros) IPv4 address.

simplesect.para: Any IPv4 address.

briefdescription.para: Return pointer to broadcast (all bits ones) IPv4 address.

simplesect.para: Broadcast IPv4 address.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given address belongs to same subnet that has been configured for the interface.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an interface

simplesect.para: True if address is in same subnet, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given IPv4 address is a broadcast address.

parameterdescription.para: Interface to use. Must be a valid pointer to an interface.

simplesect.para: True if address is a broadcast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv4 address is assigned to any network interface in the system.

parameterdescription.para: A valid pointer on an IPv4 address

simplesect.para: True if IPv4 address is found in one of the network interfaces, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is unspecified (all bits zero)

simplesect.para: True if the address is unspecified, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is solicited node multicast address FF02:0:0:0:0:1:FFXX:XXXX defined in RFC 3513.

simplesect.para: True if the address is solicited node address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a given scope multicast address (FFyx::).

simplesect.para: True if the address is in given scope multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 addresses have the same multicast scope (FFyx::).

simplesect.para: True if both addresses have same multicast scope, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Returns the scope of the given IPv6 address.

simplesect.para: Scope of the address, -1 if address is not multicast.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a global multicast address (FFxE::/16).

simplesect.para: True if the address is global multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a interface scope multicast address (FFx1::).

simplesect.para: True if the address is a interface scope multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a link local scope multicast address (FFx2::).

simplesect.para: True if the address is a link local scope multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a mesh-local scope multicast address (FFx3::).

simplesect.para: True if the address is a mesh-local scope multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a site scope multicast address (FFx5::).

simplesect.para: True if the address is a site scope multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is an organization scope multicast address (FFx8::).

simplesect.para: True if the address is an organization scope multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address belongs to certain multicast group.

parameterdescription.para: Group id IPv6 address, the values must be in network byte order

simplesect.para: True if the IPv6 multicast address belongs to given multicast group, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address belongs to the all nodes multicast group.

simplesect.para: True if the IPv6 multicast address belongs to the all nodes multicast group, false otherwise

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a interface scope all nodes multicast address (FF01::1).

simplesect.para: True if the address is a interface scope all nodes multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check if the IPv6 address is a link local scope all nodes multicast address (FF02::1).

simplesect.para: True if the address is a link local scope all nodes multicast address, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Create solicited node IPv6 multicast address FF02:0:0:0:0:1:FFXX:XXXX defined in RFC 3513.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_in6_addr *addr, uint16_t addr0, uint16_t addr1, uint16_t addr2, uint16_t addr3, uint16_t addr4, uint16_t addr5, uint16_t addr6, uint16_t addr7)
memberdef.name: net_ipv6_addr_create

briefdescription.para: Construct an IPv6 address from eight 16-bit words.

parameterdescription.para: 16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address
16-bit word which is part of the address

briefdescription.para: Create link local allnodes multicast IPv6 address.
Create link local allrouters multicast IPv6 address.
Create IPv4 mapped IPv6 address.
Is the IPv6 address an IPv4 mapped one.

detaileddescription.para: The v4 mapped addresses look like ::ffff:a.b.c.d

simplesect.para: True if IPv6 address is a IPv4 mapped address, False otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Get the IPv4 address from an IPv4 mapped IPv6 address.

detaileddescription.para: The v4 mapped addresses look like ::ffff:a.b.c.d

parameterdescription.para: IPv6 address (must be a v4-mapped address, see net_ipv6_addr_is_v4_mapped())
IPv4 address to be filled in

memberdef.argsstring: (struct net_if *iface, const struct net_in6_addr *prefix, uint8_t *network_id, size_t network_id_len, uint8_t dad_counter, struct net_in6_addr *addr, struct net_linkaddr *lladdr)
memberdef.name: net_ipv6_addr_generate_iid

briefdescription.para: Generate IPv6 address using a prefix and interface identifier.

detaileddescription.para: Interface identifier is either generated from EUI-64 (MAC) defined in RFC 4291 or from randomized value defined in RFC 7217.

parameterdescription.para: IPv6 prefix, can be left out in which case fe80::/64 is used
Network identifier (for example SSID in WLAN), this is optional can be set to NULL
Network identifier length, if set to 0 then the network id is ignored.
Duplicate Address Detection counter value, can be set to 0 if it is not known.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, < 0 if error

briefdescription.para: Create IPv6 address interface identifier.
Check if given address is based on link layer address.

simplesect.para: True if it is, False otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr from net_sockaddr_storage.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code calling this function can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to socket address (struct sockaddr)

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr_in6 from net_sockaddr.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code calling this function can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv6 socket address

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr_in from net_sockaddr.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code calling this function can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv4 socket address

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr_in6_ptr from net_sockaddr_ptr.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code calling this function can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv6 socket address

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr_in_ptr from sockaddr_ptr.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code calling this function can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to IPv4 socket address

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr_ll_ptr from sockaddr_ptr.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code calling this function can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to linklayer socket address

briefdescription.para: Get net_sockaddr_can_ptr from net_sockaddr_ptr.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper so that the code needing this functionality can be made shorter.

simplesect.para: Pointer to CAN socket address

briefdescription.para: Convert a string to IP address.

parameterdescription.para: IP address in a null terminated string
Pointer to struct net_in_addr if family is NET_AF_INET or pointer to struct net_in6_addr if family is NET_AF_INET6

simplesect.para: This function doesn't do precise error checking, do not use for untrusted strings.
0 if ok, < 0 if error

memberdef.argsstring: (net_sa_family_t family, const void *src, char *dst, size_t size)
memberdef.name: net_addr_ntop

briefdescription.para: Convert IP address to string form.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to struct net_in_addr if family is NET_AF_INET or pointer to struct net_in6_addr if family is NET_AF_INET6
Buffer for IP address as a null terminated string
Number of bytes available in the buffer

simplesect.para: dst pointer if ok, NULL if error

briefdescription.para: Create netmask from mask length.

parameterdescription.para: Netmask length (in IPv4) or prefix length (in IPv6)
Pointer to struct sockaddr_in if family is NET_AF_INET or pointer to struct sockaddr_in6 if family is NET_AF_INET6

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, < 0 if error

briefdescription.para: Create mask length from netmask.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to struct sockaddr_in if family is NET_AF_INET or pointer to struct sockaddr_in6 if family is NET_AF_INET6
Netmask length (in IPv4) or prefix length (in IPv6)

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, < 0 if error

briefdescription.para: Parse a string that contains either IPv4 or IPv6 address and optional port, and store the information in user supplied sockaddr struct.

detaileddescription.para: Syntax of the IP address string: 192.0.2.1:80 192.0.2.42

[2001:db8::2] 2001:db::42 Note that the str_len parameter is used to restrict the amount of characters that are checked. If the string does not contain port number, then the port number in sockaddr is not modified.

parameterdescription.para: String that contains the IP address.
Length of the string to be parsed.
Pointer to user supplied struct sockaddr.

simplesect.para: True if parsing could be done, false otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *str, size_t str_len, struct net_sockaddr *addr, uint8_t *mask_len)
memberdef.name: net_ipaddr_parse_mask

briefdescription.para: Parse a string that contains either IPv4 or IPv6 address and optional mask len, and store the information in user supplied sockaddr struct.

detaileddescription.para: There can be multiple IP addresses separated by comma or space. The function returns the pointer to the next IP address in the string.

Syntax of the IP address string: 192.0.2.1/24 192.0.2.42 2001:db8::1/64 2001:db8::2 2001:db::42/128 2001:db8::1/64,192.0.2.1,2001:db8::2,192.0.2.2/24 2001:db8::1/64 192.0.2.1 2001:db8::2 192.0.2.2/24 Note that the str_len parameter is used to restrict the amount of characters that are checked.

parameterdescription.para: String that contains the IP address.
Length of the string to be parsed.
Pointer to user supplied struct sockaddr.
Pointer to mask_len which is returned to the caller.

simplesect.para: NULL if there was an error while parsing. "" if we could parse the IP address and there is nothing more to parse. All other values point to next character after the "," or " " in the string.

briefdescription.para: Set the default port in the sockaddr structure.

detaileddescription.para: If the port is already set, then do nothing.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to user supplied struct sockaddr.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Compare TCP sequence numbers.

detaileddescription.para: This function compares TCP sequence numbers, accounting for wraparound effects.

simplesect.para: < 0 if seq1 < seq2, 0 if seq1 == seq2, > 0 if seq > seq2

briefdescription.para: Check that one TCP sequence number is greater.

detaileddescription.para: This is convenience function on top of net_tcp_seq_cmp().

simplesect.para: True if seq > seq2

briefdescription.para: Convert a string of hex values to array of bytes.

detaileddescription.para: The syntax of the string is "ab:02:98:fa:42:01"

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to memory where the bytes are written.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Convert Tx network packet priority to traffic class so we can place the packet into correct Tx queue.

simplesect.para: Tx traffic class that handles that priority network traffic.

briefdescription.para: Convert Rx network packet priority to traffic class so we can place the packet into correct Rx queue.

simplesect.para: Rx traffic class that handles that priority network traffic.

briefdescription.para: Convert network packet VLAN priority to network packet priority so we can place the packet into correct queue.

simplesect.para: Network priority

briefdescription.para: Convert network packet priority to network packet VLAN priority.

simplesect.para: VLAN priority (PCP)

briefdescription.para: Return network address family value as a string.

detaileddescription.para: This is only usable for debugging.

simplesect.para: Network address family as a string, or NULL if family is unknown.

briefdescription.para: Return network address size for a given family.

simplesect.para: Network address size, or 0 if family is unknown.

briefdescription.para: Add IPv6 prefix as a privacy extension filter.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the filters can either allow or deny listing.

parameterdescription.para: Tells if this filter is for allowing or denying listing.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Delete IPv6 prefix from privacy extension filter list.

simplesect.para: 0 if ok, <0 if error

briefdescription.para: Unspecified protocol family.
IP protocol family version 4.
IP protocol family version 6.
Packet family.
Controller Area Network.
Network management info.
Inter-process communication
Inter-process communication
Unspecified address family.
IP protocol family version 4.
IP protocol family version 6.
Packet family.
Controller Area Network.
Network management info.
Inter-process communication
Inter-process communication
Convert 16-bit value from network to host byte order.

parameterdescription.para: The network byte order value to convert.

simplesect.para: Host byte order value.

briefdescription.para: Convert 32-bit value from network to host byte order.

parameterdescription.para: The network byte order value to convert.

simplesect.para: Host byte order value.

briefdescription.para: Convert 64-bit value from network to host byte order.

parameterdescription.para: The network byte order value to convert.

simplesect.para: Host byte order value.

briefdescription.para: Convert 16-bit value from host to network byte order.

parameterdescription.para: The host byte order value to convert.

simplesect.para: Network byte order value.

briefdescription.para: Convert 32-bit value from host to network byte order.

parameterdescription.para: The host byte order value to convert.

simplesect.para: Network byte order value.

briefdescription.para: Convert 64-bit value from host to network byte order.

parameterdescription.para: The host byte order value to convert.

simplesect.para: Network byte order value.

briefdescription.para: Binary size of the IPv6 address.
Binary size of the IPv4 address.
Returns a pointer to the first cmsghdr in the ancillary data buffer associated with the passed msghdr.

detaileddescription.para: It returns NULL if there isn't enough space for a cmsghdr in the buffer.

memberdef.name: NET_CMSG_NXTHDR
memberdef.initializer: (((cmsg) == NULL) ? NET_CMSG_FIRSTHDR(msghdr) : \ (((uint8_t *)(cmsg) + NET_ALIGN_H((cmsg)->cmsg_len) + \ NET_ALIGN_D(sizeof(struct net_cmsghdr)) > \ (uint8_t *)((msghdr)->msg_control) + (msghdr)->msg_controllen) ? \ NULL : \ (struct net_cmsghdr *)((uint8_t *)(cmsg) + \ NET_ALIGN_H((cmsg)->cmsg_len))))

briefdescription.para: Returns the next valid cmsghdr after the passed cmsghdr.

detaileddescription.para: It returns NULL when there isn't enough space left in the buffer.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the data portion of a cmsghdr.

detaileddescription.para: The pointer returned cannot be assumed to be suitably aligned for accessing arbitrary payload data types. Applications should not cast it to a pointer type matching the payload, but should instead use memcpy(3) to copy data to or from a suitably declared object.

briefdescription.para: Returns the number of bytes an ancillary element with payload of the passed data length occupies.
Returns the value to store in the cmsg_len member of the net_cmsghdr structure, taking into account any necessary alignment.

detaileddescription.para: It takes the data length as an argument.

briefdescription.para: IPv6 address initializer.
IPv6 loopback address initializer.
IPv4 any address.
IPv4 broadcast address.
IPv4 address initializer.
IPv6 loopback address initializer.
Max length of the IPv4 address as a string.

detaileddescription.para: Defined by POSIX.

briefdescription.para: Max length of the IPv6 address as a string.

detaileddescription.para: Takes into account possible mapped IPv4 addresses.

briefdescription.para: How many priority values there are.
Copy an IPv4 or IPv6 address.

simplesect.para: Destination address.

briefdescription.para: IPv4/IPv6 primitives and helpers.
